title: Modified Request: EEOC Correspondence with Opexus

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Modified Request: EEOC Correspondence with Opexus
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is narrowed request relative to my first request about the same matter. Narrowed Request: For dates January 1, 2022 to February 28, 2026, I request all correspondence between EEOC and Opexus management and staff - including staff and management from AINS, which is the former company name of Opexus. 

Background: 
On March 3, 2026, I responded to EEOC's February 26, 2026 acknowledgment notice (or as stated by EEOC to be on February 27, 2026) and stated: "Regarding # 820-2026-005851, I thank you for the response. I do not have an account with EEOC's FOIA Office. As a standing request, ask that EEOC not require me to log into a potentially compromised FOIA system to view any updates , etc. This is especially salient because of recent serious security concerns.... Thank you for considering my requests and desire for secure and transparent communications." Again I do not  have an account with EEOC's FOIA portal and I elect not to use it for reasons I have explained. I request that all communications  occur outside of the EEOC FOIA platform and be transparently sent for public view.

On April 9, 2026, EEOC Office of Legal Counsel sent a letter stating among other things, "This letter is to inform you that we would like to have a conversation with you in hopes of clarifying and narrowing your request. We conducted a search on February 27, 2026, based on your request as written, which resulted in over 124,000 items. We do not have an exact page count, but assuming that doing an industry standard of about three (3)pages per item, the actual page count is going to be likely around the neighborhood of over 372,000 pages. We are putting this request on hold so that we may have a conversation with you concerning how you can effectively limit the scope of this request, so that it can be processed. " Unfortunately EEOC only provided me with 10 days to respond. I was on annual leave during these 10 days. Thus, I am resubmitting this modified request.  

Other Requests and Notices:
As a standing request please provide at least 20 business days for me to respond to any correspondence. In the future, please do not close any of my requests if I do not respond about narrowing a request and continue to process the request until I am able to respond. I have a reasonable accommodations request and ask that communication occur only in writing. This is a standing request. A phone call is not a good option for me. Also, I will always remain interested in receiving all requests until they are received.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jessica Elm

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

The Administrator’s Workforce Directive: Restoring NASA’s Core Competencies (or similar title), issued on or around January–March 2026.

title: Mark Middleton Alpha File Folder-Title Index

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

An administrative folder-title index for Mark Middleton records in the Chief of Staff subgroup of Clinton Presidential Records.

Specifically, I request:

1. A list of all folder titles in the Mark Middleton series, including but not limited to OA/ID 4504, where the folder title contains the convention "[Alpha File]" or "[Alpha Files]".

2. For each matching folder title, the OA/ID number, box number, folder number if available, collection or record group, subgroup or office of origin, series or staff member, and any NARA or Clinton Library catalog identifier.

3. If a complete Middleton-series folder-title index already exists, a copy of that index or the portion covering all Mark Middleton Alpha File folders.

4. If the Library maintains an electronic folder-title list for the Middleton series, please provide that list as exported or in its available native format, such as CSV, finding-aid PDF, or another existing format, rather than re-keying folder titles manually.

5. If OA/ID 4504 is not the only Mark Middleton OA/ID in this staff series, a list of the adjacent or related Mark Middleton OA/ID numbers and their high-level descriptions.

I am requesting folder-title and administrative index information only, not the full contents of the folders at this stage.

Identifying information:

  - Known folder title variants: "[Alpha File] - Jeffrey Epstein" and "[Alpha Files] - Jeffrey Epstein [OA/ID 4504]"
  - Known OA/ID number: 4504
  - Known NARA accession identifier: #3164
  - Known collection / record group: Clinton Presidential Records
  - Known subgroup / office of origin: Chief of Staff
  - Known series / staff member: Mark Middleton
  - Known related FOIA: 2019-0820-F Segment 1
  - Known Clinton Digital Library item: https://clinton.presidentiallibraries.us/items/show/118574

Prior Library correspondence identifies a responsive folder using closely related descriptions: the April 10, 2026 response in FOIA case 2026-0425-F referred to Mark Middleton files, Box 2 (OA #4504, NARA #3164), while a later Library email transmitting the scanned folder documents referred to "[Alpha Files] - Jeffrey Epstein [OA/ID 4504]" in Box 4 of 2019-0820-F Segment 1 Mark Middleton. I am including both descriptions only to help identify the relevant Mark Middleton series and related folder-title/index records.

This request seeks the folder-title index only, so that any future substantive request can be narrowly targeted to specific Alpha File folders and avoid unnecessary review burden.

I request a waiver of all search and duplication fees under 5 U.S.C. section 552(a)(4)(A)(iii), because disclosure is in the public interest and is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of government operations, archival processing, and Presidential records access.

If fees are expected despite this request, please notify me before incurring costs above $25.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

title: Administrative Manifest for Clinton Library FOIA 2019-0820-F

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

An administrative manifest for Clinton Library FOIA case 2019-0820-F, identified in the Clinton Digital Library as 2019-0820-F Segment 1.
I am requesting administrative and processing records only, not substantive record content, including:

1. The total number of segments processed to date for FOIA case 2019-0820-F.

2. For each segment, the segment number, release date, page count, collection title, and any public Clinton Digital Library item URL or identifier.

3. The current status of any segments still pending, queued, referred to another agency, withdrawn, closed without records, or otherwise unresolved.

4. Any processing summary, finding aid, case log, or release manifest for FOIA case 2019-0820-F.

5. Any FOIA exemptions, Presidential Records Act restriction categories, referrals, consultations, or other processing bases recorded at the segment level.

6. If any of items 1-5 above are already covered by the existing public finding aid at https://clinton.presidentiallibraries.us/items/show/118574, titled "Mark Middleton - Collection Finding Aid," or any related public Clinton Library item, please direct me to those resources and provide only the gaps not covered by published material.

7. If any portion of the information requested above is more efficiently provided through Library reference services rather than formal FOIA processing, I welcome that channel and consent to forwarding this request to reference staff. A reference response in lieu of formal FOIA processing is acceptable for items 1-5.

Identifying information:

- Known Clinton Digital Library item: https://clinton.presidentiallibraries.us/items/show/118574
- Item title: Mark Middleton - Collection Finding Aid
- Identifier: 2019-0820-F Segment 1
- Subject: Middleton, Mark E.
- Collection: Finding Aids - Collection Descriptions & Inventories
- Publisher: William J. Clinton Presidential Library & Museum

This request is intended to clarify the administrative scope of 2019-0820-F before filing any broader content request. A manifest will show whether additional segments exist, whether they are already public, and whether follow-on requests should target only gaps instead of duplicating already-processed material.

I request a waiver of all search and duplication fees under 5 U.S.C. section 552(a)(4)(A)(iii), because disclosure is in the public interest and is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of government operations, archival processing, and Presidential records access.

If fees are expected despite this request, please notify me before incurring costs above $25.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: German 9/11 Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I'm looking for documents/ interviews either done by the FBI or collected by the FBI from the Bundeskriminalamt (BKA) regarding 9/11 hijacker Mohamed Atta. 

Specific BKA interview reports, and witness interviews, and any related documents should involve Uwe and Doris Michaels, the married German couple that Mohamed Atta first stayed with in Hamburg, Germany.
Uwe Michaels was born on April 18, 1936 and died on Feb. 21, 2013
Doris Michaels was born on May 28, 1937, unknown death date.
Both Uwe and Doris Michaels are not U.S. Citizens and aren't protected under the Privacy Act.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Charles Kallas

title: White House Office Records Regarding Digital Infrastructure Resilience and the September 2010 Facebook outage

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: White House Office Records Regarding Digital Infrastructure Resilience and the September 2010 Facebook outage
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I hereby request access to and copies of records from the files of the National Economic Council (NEC), the Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP), and the Council of Economic Advisers (CEA) relating to federal assessment of private digital infrastructure vulnerabilities and any economic analysis conducted in connection with the internet service disruption of September 23–24, 2010.

For context: the September 2010 Facebook outage generated massive news coverage. The more than 2 hour outage also has a cascading impact on over 350,000 other websites that were dependent on Facebook's API. 

Some of the coverage from that day: 

https://engineering.fb.com/2010/09/23/uncategorized/more-details-on-today-s-outage/
https://medium.com/@kanishks772/the-day-facebook-died-a-cache-stampede-horror-story-that-changed-tech-forever-f7ee2dc4f627
https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2010/sep/24/facebook-server-error-solution
https://techcrunch.com/2010/09/23/facebook-downtime/

II. Scope of Search
I request that archivists search for the following categories of records, dated approximately August 1, 2010 through December 31, 2010:

Economic Impact Assessments: Internal memoranda, briefing materials, or analytical documents assessing productivity or commerce impacts of internet service disruptions, including but not limited to the September 2010 Facebook platform outage.

Infrastructure Vulnerability Policy Records: Documents discussing federal policy options regarding "single points of failure" in commercial internet infrastructure, digital economic resilience, or private-sector technology dependencies.

Correspondence: Emails, meeting minutes, or memoranda from (especially focusing on the the September 23rd/24th 2010 time period) involving NEC Director Lawrence Summers, Deputy Director Jason Furman, or Federal CTO Aneesh Chopra containing any of the following search terms: Facebook, internet outage, web disruption, technology infrastructure, digital economy, API, internet reliability, web centralization, Mark Zurckerberg. 

Interagency Communications: Any records reflecting coordination between the NEC, OSTP, CEA, Department of Commerce, or DHS regarding the economic implications of large-scale commercial internet disruptions.

III. Historical Justification
The period of 2009–2012 represents a formative era in federal digital policy, during which the Obama administration was simultaneously promoting e-government initiatives and grappling with the emerging economic centrality of private internet platforms. Records from this period documenting how senior economic advisers understood the systemic risks posed by private-sector digital infrastructure concentration would make a material contribution to the historical record of U.S. technology governance. This research is intended to support the public interest in highlighting the nation's economic dependence on one company. 

If certain parts of this request or certain portions must be redacted please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. In plain English: there is value in seeing, line-by-line, what's being redacted and what exemptions are being claimed.

Thank you for the Obama Library's commitment to transparency, and I look forward to working together on this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Data Sharing Fields with Accurint/PSDEX - Maplewood PD (MN)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Technical Specifications: Any Interface Control Document (ICD) or technical implementation guide that defines the data structure and data elements for the automated export of Records Management System (RMS) or Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) data to the LexisNexis "PSDEX private container".
2) Data Mapping: Any "Data Translation" guides, field-mapping tables, or schemas used to configure the transfer of local agency data into the LexisNexis Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX) or Virtual Crime Center (VCC) platforms.
3) Field Lists: Any record or "Schedule A" line item defining the "Database Interface" capabilities, specifically identifying which categories of information (e.g., Victim PII, Witness Contact Data, Real-Time Jail Bookings) are included in the nightly (or regular) automated feed.

Exclusion Note: We are not seeking URLs, passwords, network IP addresses, encryption keys, sensitive security information, or PII. We are seeking only the field name and structure of the data elements (e.g., "Name," "DOB," "SSN," "Narrative") that have been approved for mass transfer to LexisNexis/PSDEX.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

title: Accurint, PSDEX, and AVCC Data Sharing Audit - Downer's Grove PD (IL)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Accurint, PSDEX, and AVCC Data Sharing Audit - Downer's Grove PD (IL)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Request: All audit logs, usage reports, or 'search hit' notifications from LexisNexis Risk Solutions (or regarding the Accurint Virtual Crime Center/PSDEX) that show searches performed by external agencies (including but not limited to DHS, ICE, or CBP) against records originated from your department. 

---
Context: Your agency appears to have an active contract with LexisNexis Risk Solutions, which includes a signed Schedule A: "Accurint Virtual Crime Center Online Subscription." That agreement includes Addendum, Section I: Cooperation with FOIA Compliance, which reads "Notwithstanding anything in the Proposal to the contrary, Vendor acknowledges that the Freedom of Information Act 5 TLCS 1401 et. seq. (*Act”) applies to the Proposal and the Act may apply to public records in possession of the Vendor. Vendor shall cooperate with the Village in its efforts to comply with the Act." Our reporting seeks to understand whether your agency contributes data to AVCC/PSDEX, and if so, how that data is being used and shared with other agencies by LexisNexis Risk Solutions. An audit of queries within LexisNexis (Accurint/AVCC) against data generated and contributed by your agency will answer this question.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Adelanto Detention Center incident reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pertaining to the Adelanto ICE Processing Center (APC) in Adelanto, California, and to incidents reported by ERO Los Angeles concerning that facility, during the period September 15, 2025, through November 20, 2025:

1. The complete Significant Incident Reports (SIRs), including all narrative fields, attachments, supplemental reports, use-of-force reports, after-action reports, and any associated medical, video, or photographic records, for the following four use-of-force incidents at Adelanto APC:

   a. SIR No. 2025SIR0019621 — use of force on a Nicaraguan national detainee, September 29, 2025;
   b. SIR No. 2026SIR0000149 — use of force on a Dominican national detainee, October 2, 2025;
   c. SIR No. 2026SIR0001902 — use of force on a Salvadoran national detainee, October 30, 2025;
   d. SIR No. 2026SIR0002370 — use of force on a Nicaraguan national detainee, November 6, 2025.

2. All other Significant Incident Reports — including but not limited to use-of-force incidents, in-custody injuries, in-custody deaths, suicide attempts or self-harm, sexual assault, medical emergencies, hunger strikes, and disturbances — generated at or concerning Adelanto APC during the period September 15, 2025, through November 20, 2025.

3. Any logs, indices, or registers of significant incidents at Adelanto APC for the same period.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

CJ Ciaramella

title: Massachusetts Public Records Request – Prince Lobel Tye LLP Engagement

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Massachusetts Public Records Request – Prince Lobel Tye LLP Engagement
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to and copies of records pertaining to the MCCA’s retention of the law firm Prince Lobel Tye LLP for the workplace investigation that concluded in October 2023.

For reference, the final report produced from this engagement, titled "Report on Investigation," is dated October 13, 2023, and is currently hosted on the MCCA’s AWS server here: https://s3.amazonaws.com/massconvention/pdf/Public_Records/Report_on_Investigation_-_10.19.23.pdf 

Specifically, I am requesting the following documents:

The Contract / Engagement Letter: The fully executed engagement letter, contract, or retention agreement establishing the scope of work and financial terms between the MCCA and Prince Lobel Tye LLP for this investigation.

Conflict Waivers: Any and all conflict of interest waivers (including advance waivers for future representation) requested by Prince Lobel Tye LLP and executed by the MCCA or its representatives.

Invoices and Billing Records: All invoices, billing statements, and payment records submitted by Prince Lobel Tye LLP to the MCCA related to this engagement, from the date of retention through the present.

Please search for records after 2020 to present.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brian McCarter

title: Intra-system transfers in ICE custody June 2025 to April 2026

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Intra-system transfers in ICE custody June 2025 to April 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records of intra-system transfers in ICE custody from the Wyatt Detention Facility to facilities where ICE detainees are held, processed or transported between June 2025 and January 2026 inclusive. Records of transfers may include correspondence regarding transfers, confirmation of transfers, and transfer orders. Records should include dates of transfers, numbers of people transferred on each date, and facility names of destination of transfers.

Please additionally provide any documentation of transfers in ICE custody between February and April 2026. 

The prior request returned a .zip file with no document referencing transfers in ICE custody in any manner. Please provide a response to this inquiry.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Dunwoody RTCC Badge Logs February 12th 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All electronic access logs, keycard entry logs, badge reader logs, or any other records generated by the access control system at the Dunwoody Police Department Real Time Crime Center on February 12, 2026, between 8:00am and 12:00pm Eastern Time.

This request includes all records showing any individual who entered or attempted to enter the Real Time Crime Center during that time window, including but not limited to the identity of the individual, the time of entry, and the credential used to gain access.

For context, the City of Dunwoody's Communications Director has stated in writing that on the morning of February 12, 2026, Lieutenant Timothy Fecht gave a tour of the Real Time Crime Center to representatives of Flock Safety. I am requesting the access logs to confirm which individuals accessed the Real Time Crime Center during that time period.

For purposes of this request, "records" includes any electronically stored data generated by the access control system, whether maintained by the City of Dunwoody directly or by a third-party vendor on the city's behalf.

Pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1), I request that responsive records be provided in their native electronic format with full metadata preserved, including but not limited to timestamps and user identification data.

If any portion of this request is denied, I request that you:
(1) Identify each responsive record being withheld with sufficient detail to permit me to assess the basis for withholding;
(2) Cite the specific statutory exemption(s) claimed for each record or portion thereof; and
(3) Pursuant to the segregability requirement of O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72(a)(44), produce all non-exempt portions of partially exempt records with redactions clearly marked and justified on a field-by-field basis.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Dunwoody Suitet 239 Access Logs  February 12th 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All electronic access logs, keycard entry logs, badge reader logs, or any other records generated by the access control system at 4800 Ashford Dunwoody Rd NE, Suite 239, Dunwoody, GA 30338 (Dunwoody Police Department lobby) on February 12, 2026, between 8:00am and 12:00pm Eastern Time.

This request includes all records showing any individual who entered or attempted to enter Suite 239 during that time window, including but not limited to the identity of the individual, the time of entry, and the credential used to gain access.

For purposes of this request, "records" includes any electronically stored data generated by the access control system, whether maintained by the City of Dunwoody directly or by a third-party vendor on the city's behalf.

Pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1), I request that responsive records be provided in their native electronic format with full metadata preserved, including but not limited to timestamps and user identification data.

If any portion of this request is denied, I request that you:
(1) Identify each responsive record being withheld with sufficient detail to permit me to assess the basis for withholding;
(2) Cite the specific statutory exemption(s) claimed for each record or portion thereof; and
(3) Pursuant to the segregability requirement of O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72(a)(44), produce all non-exempt portions of partially exempt records with redactions clearly marked and justified on a field-by-field basis.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

communications.subject: Indiana Access to Public Records Act Request: Hope Parks Homicide Investigation – August 2020
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Indiana Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Indiana Access to Public Records Act (Ind. Code § 5-14-3-1 et seq.), I request the following records relating to the homicide investigation of Hope Parks (August 2020), involving Michael Dale Parks. The case is fully closed following his guilty plea and 50-year sentence in May 2022.
I am specifically requesting:
	1.	Interview 1 (recorded police interview with Michael Dale Parks)
	2.	Interview 2 (recorded police interview with Michael Dale Parks)
	3.	Jail Interview (recorded interview with Michael Dale Parks conducted post-arrest)
I have already been in contact with the Crawfordsville Police Department records department and have received a price sheet quoting $150.00 per video for these three items ($450.00 total). I am based in the United Kingdom and unable to pay via cash or money order. I am requesting assistance processing this payment and obtaining these records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Baruch Gideon Appahoh
